_comment: Cleanup catalog. method=trash → moved to Trash (recoverable). IMPORTANT ordering rule: any target whose path is a DEPTH-1 dir under ~/Library/Caches or ~/Library/Logs MUST appear BEFORE user-caches / user-logs, so the scanner's seen-set claims it and the generic catch-all does not double-count its bytes. Verified by a swarm with web checks (2026-06-19).

targets.name: Xcode DerivedData
targets.note: Rebuildable Xcode build cache.

targets.name: Xcode Device Support
targets.note: Old device symbols (often 5-15 GB). The newest OS version per platform is kept automatically; only older ones are offered. Re-downloaded when a device connects.

targets.name: Xcode Archives
targets.note: Needed for re-export/dSYM. Delete deliberately.

targets.name: Xcode Device Logs
targets.note: Device logs and documentation cache — regenerated.

targets.name: CoreSimulator Caches
targets.note: Simulator caches only; simulator data is left untouched.

targets.name: Swift Package Manager Clones
targets.note: Cloned package repositories and checkouts (~/Library/org.swift.swiftpm). Re-cloned on next build.

targets.name: Xcode SwiftUI Previews
targets.note: Cached preview simulators and data for SwiftUI Previews. Regenerated by Xcode — can be several GB.

targets.name: Parallels Caches
targets.note: Parallels Desktop app/console caches. Regenerated automatically; NEVER contains VM disks (.pvm/.hdd live under ~/Parallels).

targets.name: JetBrains IDE Caches
targets.note: Per-product caches/indexes for all JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, Rider, CLion, DataGrip, etc.). Rebuilt on next project open. (Also holds Local History — clear deliberately.) Config/plugins live elsewhere and are untouched.

targets.name: Google Tool Caches
targets.note: Regenerable caches under ~/Library/Caches/Google (Android Studio indexes, Firebase, gcloud SDK component cache, etc.). Re-created on next launch. Settings, SDKs and ~/Library/Android are untouched.

targets.name: Visual Studio for Mac Cache
targets.note: Cache for the retired Visual Studio for Mac IDE (debug symbols, MEF/component caches). Rebuilt on demand; often pure leftover since VS for Mac is end-of-life.

targets.name: Microsoft Office Caches
targets.note: Office app caches under ~/Library/Caches (Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook/OneNote, AutoUpdate, error reporting). Pure regenerable cache; mail/documents/accounts live elsewhere and are untouched.

targets.name: Homebrew Download Cache
targets.note: Downloaded bottles, formulae and Cask installers Homebrew keeps to speed reinstalls (what `brew cleanup -s` prunes). Re-downloaded on demand. The install at /opt/homebrew is untouched.

targets.name: CocoaPods Cache
targets.note: Local download mirror of fetched pods (`pod cache clean --all`). Re-downloaded on next `pod install`; your project Pods/ and Podfile are untouched.

targets.name: Carthage Cache
targets.note: Carthage's cloned dependency repos and prebuilt binaries. Re-cloned/rebuilt on next `carthage update`.

targets.name: node-gyp Cache
targets.note: Downloaded Node.js headers per version, used to compile native addons. Re-downloaded on next native build. (macOS default ~/Library/Caches/node-gyp; XDG fallback ~/.cache/node-gyp.)

targets.name: Electron Download Cache
targets.note: Downloaded Electron release zips per version (@electron/get). Re-downloaded on next install/build. Honors $electron_config_cache / $ELECTRON_CACHE.

targets.name: electron-builder Cache
targets.note: Downloaded build-toolset archives (code-sign tools, app-builder, nsis, etc.). Re-downloaded on next build. Honors $ELECTRON_BUILDER_CACHE.

targets.name: Playwright Browser Cache
targets.note: Downloaded browser binaries (Chromium/Firefox/WebKit) for Playwright — hundreds of MB to GB. Re-downloaded with `npx playwright install`.

targets.name: Cypress Cache
targets.note: Downloaded Cypress test-runner binaries (one per version). Re-downloaded on next install. Cypress app data/settings live elsewhere.

targets.name: ripgrep-all Cache
targets.note: Extracted-text database rga builds to speed repeat searches inside PDFs/office/archives. Pure performance cache; rebuilt on demand.

targets.name: Pipenv Cache
targets.note: Pipenv download and built-wheel cache. Re-fetched on next install. Honors $PIPENV_CACHE_DIR.

targets.name: Black Formatter Cache
targets.note: Black records already-formatted files to skip re-formatting. Fully regenerable. Honors $BLACK_CACHE_DIR. (Moderate: $BLACK_CACHE_DIR is user-set, so not auto-selected.)

targets.name: User Caches
targets.note: All other user app caches (~/Library/Caches). Regenerated. Itemized caches above are counted separately.

targets.name: Homebrew Logs
targets.note: Per-formula build/install and brew services logs. Regenerated on next install/service run.

targets.name: User Logs
targets.note: All other user logs and crash reports (~/Library/Logs).

targets.name: Saved Application State
targets.note: Apps may open without their previous window state.

targets.name: npx Cache
targets.note: Packages npx downloads to run commands (~/.npm/_npx). Re-downloaded on next npx run.

targets.name: Bun Cache
targets.note: Bun's global install cache. Re-downloaded from the registry on next install — moderate (full re-fetch).

targets.name: Rust / Cargo Cache
targets.note: Downloaded crates and registry index. Re-fetched on next build. Honors $CARGO_HOME.

targets.name: Go Module Cache
targets.note: Downloaded Go modules. Re-downloaded on next build. Honors $GOMODCACHE / $GOPATH. (Go build cache lives under ~/Library/Caches → User Caches.)

targets.name: pnpm Store
targets.note: pnpm content-addressable store. Re-fetched on next install (existing node_modules may need reinstall). Honors $PNPM_HOME.

targets.name: Maven Repository Cache
targets.note: Maven's local repository of downloaded dependency JARs/POMs (~/.m2/repository). Re-downloaded on next build. Note: may also hold locally-installed SNAPSHOTs that are NOT re-downloadable — clear deliberately. settings.xml is untouched.

targets.name: Flutter / Dart pub Cache
targets.note: Dart/Flutter downloaded packages. Re-fetched on next `pub get` (or `dart pub cache repair`). Honors $PUB_CACHE. Globally-activated packages and the SDK are untouched.

targets.name: NuGet HTTP Cache
targets.note: NuGet HTTP feed cache and plugin-claims cache. Regenerated on next restore. The global packages store is separate and untouched.

targets.name: NuGet Global Packages
targets.note: Downloaded .NET packages (~/.nuget/packages). PackageReference projects use these directly; removing forces a re-restore. Honors $NUGET_PACKAGES. NuGet.Config credentials are untouched.

targets.name: SDKMAN Archives
targets.note: SDKMAN's cache of downloaded SDK zip archives (`sdk flush archives`). Installed SDKs in candidates/ are untouched.

targets.name: Puppeteer Browser Cache
targets.note: Downloaded Chrome/Chromium binaries for Puppeteer (v19+). Re-downloaded on next install. Honors $PUPPETEER_CACHE_DIR.

targets.name: pre-commit Cache
targets.note: Cloned hook repos and built hook environments for pre-commit (`pre-commit clean`). Rebuilt on next run. Honors $PRE_COMMIT_HOME.

targets.name: Conda Package Index Cache
targets.note: Conda channel-index metadata (repodata) under pkgs/cache (`conda clean --index-cache`). Targets ONLY the index cache, never tarballs or extracted packages that environments hard-link to.

targets.name: Jupyter Runtime Files
targets.note: Transient Jupyter runtime files (kernel connection/pid/log files), useful only while a process runs. Regenerated on next launch. Honors $JUPYTER_RUNTIME_DIR. Installed kernelspecs are untouched.

targets.name: VS Code Caches
targets.note: Regenerable Electron/Chromium caches and logs for VS Code. Rebuilt on next launch. Settings, snippets, profiles and extensions are untouched.

targets.name: VS Code Insiders Caches
targets.note: Regenerable Electron/Chromium caches and logs for the VS Code Insiders build. Rebuilt on next launch. Settings and extensions are untouched.

targets.name: Cursor Caches
targets.note: Cursor (Electron/Chromium) render & code caches, rebuilt on launch. Settings, extensions and chat history live elsewhere and are untouched.

targets.name: Claude Code Legacy Folders
targets.note: Legacy Claude Code dirs (todos/statsig/logs) no longer written by current versions. Session transcripts, history, settings and credentials are NOT here and are untouched.

targets.name: Claude Code Paste & Image Cache
targets.note: Cached large pastes and attached images from Claude Code sessions. Regenerated as needed; don't clear mid-session. Transcripts/history are untouched.

targets.name: OpenCode Cache
targets.note: OpenCode provider-package cache (~/.cache/opencode). Re-downloaded on demand. Sessions, config and auth (~/.local/share, ~/.config) are untouched.

targets.name: Continue Logs
targets.note: Continue plugin logs (~/.continue/logs). Recreated on next run. Sessions, config and dev_data are untouched.

targets.name: Continue Codebase Index
targets.note: Continue's codebase index (embeddings + metadata) derived from your source. Fully regenerable, but re-indexing large repos takes time and may re-spend embedding API calls.

targets.name: Docker Desktop Logs
targets.note: Docker Desktop host and VM service logs. Recreated on next run. Does NOT touch images, containers, volumes or the Docker.raw disk image.

targets.name: Office Document Cache
targets.note: Office Upload Center / document sync cache. Quit Office apps and verify the Upload Center shows no pending uploads before clearing, otherwise unsynced changes can be lost. Originals are untouched.

targets.name: Office App Caches (sandbox)
targets.note: Per-app sandbox caches for Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook/OneNote. Rebuilt on next launch; documents, mail database and AutoRecovery are NOT in these folders and are untouched. Quit the apps first.

targets.name: Poetry Cache
targets.note: Poetry downloaded-package cache and built wheels. Re-fetched on next install. Honors $POETRY_CACHE_DIR. EXCLUDES virtualenvs (those are environments).

targets.name: HuggingFace cache
targets.note: HuggingFace downloaded-model cache (~/.cache/huggingface). Stored SEPARATELY from app models — deleting a model in "Large Files" does not free this. Re-downloads when needed. Can be very large. Honors $HF_HOME / $HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE.

targets.name: PyTorch cache
targets.note: PyTorch model/checkpoint cache (~/.cache/torch). Re-downloads when needed.

targets.name: Whisper Models
targets.note: Downloaded Whisper speech-to-text checkpoints (~/.cache/whisper). Re-downloaded from OpenAI's CDN on next use; moderate (large re-download).

targets.name: RubyGems Download Cache
targets.note: Re-downloadable .gem archives (the cache/ subdir only — installed gems and credentials are left untouched). Re-fetched on next gem install.

targets.name: RubyGems Source Index
targets.note: Downloaded remote source-index metadata (specs.4.8 etc., ~/.gem/specs). Re-fetched on next gem install; never touches credentials or installed gems.

targets.name: pyenv Build Cache
targets.note: python-build's cache of Python source tarballs (used only during `pyenv install`). Installed interpreters ($PYENV_ROOT/versions) are untouched.

targets.name: rustup Download Staging
targets.note: rustup's staging for downloaded component archives (extracted into ~/.rustup/toolchains, which are untouched). Re-fetched on next install/update.

targets.name: iPhone & iPad Backups
targets.note: ⚠️ Local device backups (MobileSync). These ARE your phone/tablet backups — removing them loses the backup unless you have another copy. Never auto-selected; choose deliberately. Moved to Trash (recoverable until you empty it).

targets.name: iOS / iPadOS Software Updates
targets.note: Downloaded .ipsw firmware used to update/restore devices. Re-downloaded when needed — often several GB each.

targets.name: Mail Downloads
targets.note: Temporary attachment copies opened from Mail. The originals stay in your email — these are re-downloadable.

targets.name: Incomplete Downloads
targets.note: Partial downloads — shown for review only; the app never deletes from your Downloads.

targets.name: Telegram Data Report
targets.note: Report-only. Telegram database/media are never auto-cleaned.

targets.name: Orphan LaunchAgents Report
targets.note: Report-only: user LaunchAgents pointing to missing executables.

targets.name: Process Report
targets.note: Report-only: resource-hungry/hung/orphaned processes (not zombies).

targets.name: Large Files Report
targets.note: Report-only: large files in the home folder, except protected ones.
